summary: Do not ship: the branch introduces an out-of-scope, race-prone PR-closing behavior, so the claimed single-flip release contract is false.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: Single flip violated: cached evidence can close a newly reviewed sibling PR
findings.body: The executor selects same-app, different-tag siblings and later runs `gh pr close`, although the stated flip only deduplicates the same app+tag. This is a second externally observable mutation; W34 even classifies closing as a RED→GREEN regression, so 122/122 green does not prove single-flip preservation. The close eligibility and `humanTouch` value are also cached well before the close. A review, comment, hold label, or reopen arriving before `gh pr close` does not change the head OID and will therefore be missed, allowing active work to be closed.
findings.recommendation: Remove automatic sibling closure from this bugfix and retain only auto-merge revocation. If closure is still wanted, ship it separately with explicit authorization and a fresh state/head/human-touch check immediately before the operation, preferably in an owner-approved maintenance workflow.

next_steps: Move W34 and the `gh pr close` path into a separately reviewed change.
Regenerate the RED/GREEN lock and rerun regression and characterization suites after restoring the single-flip scope.
